title: Litosanthes robinsonii

The classification of Litosanthes robinsonii is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Asterales. intro: Litosanthes robinsonii, also known as Robinson's Litosanthes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Australia and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Litosanthes robinsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Litosanthes robinsonii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
